channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Core Democratic groups are preparing to be targeted by the Trump administration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As President Donald Trump pushes the historical boundaries of executive power, some of the Democratic Party's core political institutions are preparing for the possibility that the federal government may soon launch criminal investigations against them.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House proposes drastic cuts to State Department and funding for UN, NATO and other groups
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House’s Office of Management and Budget has proposed gutting the State Department’s budget by almost 50%, closing a number of overseas diplomatic missions, slashing the number of diplomatic staff, and eliminating funding for nearly all international organizations, includi
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: DOGE uncovers massive VA contract for ‘salary survey data and analysis'
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) on Monday announced more cuts within the Department of Veterans Affairs by millions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Army to control land on Mexico border as part of base, migrants could be detained, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A long sliver of federal land along the U.S.-Mexico border that President Donald Trump is turning over to the Department of Defense would be controlled by the Army as part of a base, which could allow troops to detain any trespassers, including migrants, U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers push to meet with Salvadoran president to plead case of mistakenly deported man
item.description: Lawmakers are pushing to meet with Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele while he’s in Washington amid broader efforts to secure the return of a Maryland man wrongfully deported to a prison in the Central American nation. Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) sent a letter to El Salvador’s ambassador to the U.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he's open to sending violent criminals who are US citizens to El Salvador prison
item.description: President Trump on Monday indicated he would be open to sending American citizens who are violent criminals to El Salvador to be held in a notorious prison there, telling reporters his administration is studying law on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Man arrested in Georgia for threats against Tulsi Gabbard, her husband
item.description: Federal authorities arrested 25-year-old Aliakbar Mohammed Amin for allegedly sending threatening text messages to DNI Tulsi Gabbard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Marocco, mastermind behind dismantling of USAID projects, leaves State Department
item.description: Pete Marocco, who led the dismantling of USAID under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, departs as critics accuse him of chaos and foreign aid cuts that gutted U.S. global development programs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Despite a court order, White House bars AP from Oval Office event
item.description: The Trump administration has turned a reporter and photographer from The Associated Press away from covering an Oval Office news conference involving President Donald Trump and El Salvador President Nayib Bukele.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sanders jokingly calls Ocasio-Cortez his daughter at Utah rally
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) joked during a joint appearance at a Utah rally on Sunday that Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) was his daughter, praising the progressive lawmaker and highlighting her background.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know as Social Security anti-fraud measures take effect
item.description: Individuals who are flagged will be required to prove their identity in person for their Social Security claim to be processed.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Both the US and El Salvador refuse to return wrongly deported man to the US
item.description: President Donald Trump’s top advisers and Nayib Bukele, the president of El Salvador, said Monday that they had no basis for the small Central American nation to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland man who was wrongly deported there last month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Plans to celebrate America's 250th anniversary were underway. Then came the federal funding cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Community celebrations being planned to commemorate the nation's 250th anniversary next year are at risk of being significantly scaled back or canceled because of federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump's administration, according to multiple state humanities councils acr
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Africa appoints a special US envoy after its ambassador was expelled
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South African President Cyril Ramaphosa appointed a former deputy finance minister as a special envoy to the United States on Monday after the ambassador was expelled last month by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro and the alleged arson at his official residence
item.description: A man is facing charges after authorities say he broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion in the middle of the night, set a fire that left significant damage and forced Gov. Josh Shapiro, his family and guests to evacuate the building during the Jewish holiday of Passover.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump is 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump’s doctor says the oldest man elected president is “fully fit” to serve as commander in chief as the White House released results Sunday of Trump's recent physical exam.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bill de Blasio on what's next for the Democratic Party
item.description: De Blasio, who has received fierce criticism from President Trump this week, believes the Democrats blew the presidential election on multiple fronts.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's commerce secretary says new electronics tariff exemptions are temporary, chip tariffs coming
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tariff exemptions announced Friday on electronics like smartphones and laptops are only a temporary reprieve until the Trump administration develops a new tariff approach specific to the semiconductor industry, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Military schools offer test case for Trump education reforms
item.description: Military academies could increasingly show what President Trump wants to see from public schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump in ‘excellent health,’ doctor says after annual exam
item.description: White House physician Sean Barbabella wrote in a memo. ”His active lifestyle continues to contribute significantly to his well-being.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Will the SAVE Act make it harder for married women to vote? We ask legal experts
item.description: Legal experts say states could help married women who have changed their last names by accepting documents like a legal decree or a marriage certificate, but it might not fix the issue for all.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Trump tariffs bring Democrat disagreements to surface
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariffs are putting Democrats in a challenging political position as they decide how strongly to denounce his trade policy.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans fear Trump’s trade war could lead to political wipeout
item.description: Republican lawmakers say there’s a good chance that President Trump's trade war will boomerang on Republicans politically in 2026, as rising prices and shrinking growth could offset other accomplishments by the GOP.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump urges Congress to make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: President Donald Trump said Congress should “push hard for more Daylight at the end of a day” in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Trump admin tells judge man wrongly deported to El Salvador is alive, still detained
item.description: On Friday, federal judge Paula Xinis had ordered the Trump administration to provide daily updates to return Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Trump sits cageside at Miami UFC event in his latest appearance at a sports event
item.description: President Donald Trump said the standing ovation and cheers he drew when he took his seat at a UFC event in his home state of Florida were signs “we're doing a good job.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Russia-Ukraine talks at 'put up or shut up' point
item.description: Trump's comments came after a top envoy traveled to Russia to meet directly with President Vladimir Putin on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: White House authorizes military control of land along the border
item.description: In a historic move, the Department of Defense now has control of the Roosevelt Reservation, a 60-foot-wide federal strip.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Maher praises ‘gracious and measured’ Trump after White House visit
item.description: The talk show host emphasized that he did not turn “MAGA” during the meeting over dinner at the White House on Mar. 31.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Public bathroom access in NYC to be voted on by City Council
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Knowing where the closest bathroom is in New York City can be a challenge, but a new City Council bill may change that. Councilmember Sandy Nurse is leading the push to expand public bathroom access.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
